messages.content: 你好，我想要制作一个日语学习app，但是有ai功能，ai可以根据用户学习进度来进行安排复习和练习我主要面对完全零基础的人群，这个app的主要目的是为了帮助外国人在日本找工作的实用类。2.五十音，词汇、语法、听力、会话 都有。是综合类app 3.ai的作用是根据用户目前学习进度来制作练习语法，词汇类练习，练习的类型根据用户的正确率和学习进度来。4复习时间采用艾宾浩斯遗忘曲线 5.我自己学习日语时最大的问题是难以得到我想要的练习方式，我希望ai可以定制复习或者练习需要加强职场相关的内容，面试，简历，和职业相关单词，但是这些要等用户把基础打好才可以进行专项训练，2.如果用户某一个知识点一直错误的话，我更倾向于使用情景会话的方式来帮助用户理解 3.练习方便以翻译题为主，选择题为辅助的模式，外加一定阶段后的情景对话模拟。4.我们这个app是注重职业训练所以，用户需要先表达自己的就业倾向，之后给对应的推荐路线。基础路线五十音→基础词汇→N5语法所有路线都是固定，之后随着职业不同来改变。

你好，我想要制作一个日语学习app，但是有ai功能，ai可以根据用户学习进度来进行安排复习和练习我主要面对完全零基础的人群，这个app的主要目的是为了帮助外国人在日本找工作的实用类。2.五十音，词汇、语法、听力、会话 都有。是综合类app 3.ai的作用是根据用户目前学习进度来制作练习语法，词汇类练习，练习的类型根据用户的正确率和学习进度来。4复习时间采用艾宾浩斯遗忘曲线 5.我自己学习日语时最大的问题是难以得到我想要的练习方式，我希望ai可以定制复习或者练习需要加强职场相关的内容，面试，简历，和职业相关单词，但是这些要等用户把基础打好才可以进行专项训练，2.如果用户某一个知识点一直错误的话，我更倾向于使用情景会话的方式来帮助用户理解 3.练习方便以翻译题为主，选择题为辅助的模式，外加一定阶段后的情景对话模拟。4.我们这个app是注重职业训练所以，用户需要先表达自己的就业倾向，之后给对应的推荐路线。基础路线五十音→基础词汇→N5语法所有路线都是固定，之后随着职业不同来改变。
我想做一个比较复杂的计算器，要有加减乘除，还有余数，还有可以整除，外加展示历史记录

plan.description: Implement core arithmetic operations (add, subtract, multiply, divide) with input validation and division-by-zero error handling.

interface.name: divide
interface.description: Return the quotient of two numbers after validating inputs and ensuring divisor is non-zero.

plan.description: Add UI/handler wiring for selecting arithmetic operations and displaying results or error messages.

interface.name: handle_arithmetic_operation
interface.description: Select an arithmetic operation, execute it, and return a formatted result or error message for display.

plan.description: Implement remainder operation with divisor-zero validation.

interface.name: remainder
interface.description: Return the remainder of a divided by b after validating inputs and ensuring divisor is non-zero.

plan.description: Expose remainder operation in UI/handler flow and display results or errors.

interface.name: handle_remainder_operation
interface.description: Execute remainder operation and return a formatted result or error message for display.

plan.description: Implement divisibility check operation with divisor-zero validation.

interface.name: is_divisible
interface.description: Return True if a is divisible by b after validating inputs and ensuring divisor is non-zero.

plan.description: Expose divisibility check in UI/handler flow and display boolean result or error.

interface.name: handle_divisibility_check
interface.description: Execute divisibility check and return a formatted boolean result or error message for display.

plan.description: Create calculation history model/store and append successful calculations with expression and result.

interface.name: get_history_entries
interface.description: Return calculation history in chronological order as (expression, result) tuples.

plan.description: Render history list in UI consistently in chronological order with expression and result.

interface.name: render_history
interface.description: Render history entries into a UI-friendly string in chronological order.

requirements.title: Basic arithmetic operations
requirements.description: Provide calculator functions for addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division on user-entered numbers.
acceptance_criteria: Users can input two numbers and select addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division.
The calculator displays the correct result for each arithmetic operation.
Division by zero is handled gracefully with a clear error message instead of crashing.

requirements.title: Remainder calculation
requirements.description: Support calculating the remainder of division between two numbers.
acceptance_criteria: Users can select a remainder operation for two numeric inputs.
The calculator returns the correct remainder result.
If the divisor is zero, the calculator shows a clear error message.

requirements.title: Divisibility check
requirements.description: Allow users to check whether one number is evenly divisible by another.
acceptance_criteria: Users can input two numbers and choose a divisibility check operation.
The calculator indicates whether the first number is divisible by the second without remainder.
If the divisor is zero, the calculator shows a clear error message.

requirements.title: Calculation history
requirements.description: Display a history of completed calculations, including the expression and result, so users can review past operations.
acceptance_criteria: Each successful calculation is added to the history list.
Each history entry shows the operation performed and its result.
The history is displayed in chronological order or reverse chronological order consistently during use.
